members.name: AddBus
members.summary: Adds a bus at [param at_position].

members.name: AddBusEffect
members.signature: public void AddBusEffect(int busIdx, AudioEffect effect, int atPosition)
members.summary: Adds an [AudioEffect] effect to the bus [param bus_idx] at [param at_position].

members.name: GenerateBusLayout
members.summary: Generates an [AudioBusLayout] using the available buses and effects.

members.name: GetBusChannels
members.summary: Returns the number of channels of the bus at index [param bus_idx].

members.name: GetBusEffect
members.signature: public AudioEffect GetBusEffect(int busIdx, int effectIdx)
members.summary: Returns the [AudioEffect] at position [param effect_idx] in bus [param bus_idx].

members.name: GetBusEffectCount
members.summary: Returns the number of effects on the bus at [param bus_idx].

members.name: GetBusEffectInstance
members.signature: public AudioEffectInstance GetBusEffectInstance(int busIdx, int effectIdx, int channel)
members.summary: Returns the [AudioEffectInstance] assigned to the given bus and effect indices (and optionally channel).

members.name: GetBusIndex
members.summary: Returns the index of the bus with the name [param bus_name]. Returns [code]-1[/code] if no bus with the specified name exist.

members.name: GetBusName
members.summary: Returns the name of the bus with the index [param bus_idx].

members.name: GetBusPeakVolumeLeftDb
members.signature: public float GetBusPeakVolumeLeftDb(int busIdx, int channel)
members.summary: Returns the peak volume of the left speaker at bus index [param bus_idx] and channel index [param channel].

members.name: GetBusPeakVolumeRightDb
members.signature: public float GetBusPeakVolumeRightDb(int busIdx, int channel)
members.summary: Returns the peak volume of the right speaker at bus index [param bus_idx] and channel index [param channel].

members.name: GetBusSend
members.summary: Returns the name of the bus that the bus at index [param bus_idx] sends to.

members.name: GetBusVolumeDb
members.summary: Returns the volume of the bus at index [param bus_idx] in dB.

members.name: GetBusVolumeLinear
members.summary: Returns the volume of the bus at index [param bus_idx] as a linear value. [b]Note:[/b] The returned value is equivalent to the result of [method @GlobalScope.db_to_linear] on the result of [method get_bus_volume_db].

members.name: GetDriverName
members.summary: Returns the name of the current audio driver. The default usually depends on the operating system, but may be overridden via the [code]--audio-driver[/code] [url=$DOCS_URL/tutorials/editor/command_line_tutorial.html]command line argument[/url]. [code]--headless[/code] also automatically sets the audio driver to [code]Dummy[/code]. See also [member ProjectSettings.audio/driver/driver].

members.name: GetInputBufferLengthFrames
members.summary: Returns the absolute size of the microphone input buffer. This is set to a multiple of the audio latency and can be used to estimate the minimum rate at which the frames need to be fetched.

members.name: GetInputDeviceList
members.summary: Returns the names of all audio input devices detected on the system. [b]Note:[/b] [member ProjectSettings.audio/driver/enable_input] must be [code]true[/code] for audio input to work. See also that setting's description for caveats related to permissions and operating system privacy settings.

members.name: GetInputFrames
members.summary: Returns a [PackedVector2Array] containing exactly [param frames] audio samples from the internal microphone buffer if available, otherwise returns an empty [PackedVector2Array]. The buffer is filled at the rate of [method get_input_mix_rate] frames per second when [method set_input_device_active] has successfully been set to [code]true[/code]. The samples are signed floating-point PCM values between [code]-1[/code] and [code]1[/code].

members.name: GetInputFramesAvailable
members.summary: Returns the number of frames available to read using [method get_input_frames].

members.name: GetInputMixRate
members.summary: Returns the sample rate at the input of the [AudioServer].

members.name: GetMixRate
members.summary: Returns the sample rate at the output of the [AudioServer].

members.name: GetOutputDeviceList
members.summary: Returns the names of all audio output devices detected on the system.

members.name: GetOutputLatency
members.summary: Returns the audio driver's effective output latency. This is based on [member ProjectSettings.audio/driver/output_latency], but the exact returned value will differ depending on the operating system and audio driver. [b]Note:[/b] This can be expensive; it is not recommended to call [method get_output_latency] every frame.

members.name: GetSpeakerMode
members.summary: Returns the speaker configuration.

members.name: GetTimeSinceLastMix
members.summary: Returns the relative time since the last mix occurred, in seconds.

members.name: GetTimeToNextMix
members.summary: Returns the relative time until the next mix occurs, in seconds.

members.name: IsBusBypassingEffects
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the bus at index [param bus_idx] is bypassing effects.

members.name: IsBusEffectEnabled
members.signature: public bool IsBusEffectEnabled(int busIdx, int effectIdx)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the effect at index [param effect_idx] on the bus at index [param bus_idx] is enabled.

members.name: IsBusMute
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the bus at index [param bus_idx] is muted.

members.name: IsBusSolo
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the bus at index [param bus_idx] is in solo mode.

members.name: IsStreamRegisteredAsSample
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the stream is registered as a sample. The engine will not have to register it before playing the sample. If [code]false[/code], the stream will have to be registered before playing it. To prevent lag spikes, register the stream as sample with [method register_stream_as_sample].

members.name: Lock
members.summary: Locks the audio driver's main loop. [b]Note:[/b] Remember to unlock it afterwards.

members.name: MoveBus
members.signature: public void MoveBus(int index, int toIndex)
members.summary: Moves the bus from index [param index] to index [param to_index].

members.name: RegisterStreamAsSample
members.summary: Forces the registration of a stream as a sample. [b]Note:[/b] Lag spikes may occur when calling this method, especially on single-threaded builds. It is suggested to call this method while loading assets, where the lag spike could be masked, instead of registering the sample right before it needs to be played.

members.name: RemoveBus
members.summary: Removes the bus at index [param index].

members.name: RemoveBusEffect
members.signature: public void RemoveBusEffect(int busIdx, int effectIdx)
members.summary: Removes the effect at index [param effect_idx] from the bus at index [param bus_idx].

members.name: SetBusBypassEffects
members.signature: public void SetBusBypassEffects(int busIdx, bool enable)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the bus at index [param bus_idx] is bypassing effects.

members.name: SetBusEffectEnabled
members.signature: public void SetBusEffectEnabled(int busIdx, int effectIdx, bool enabled)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the effect at index [param effect_idx] on the bus at index [param bus_idx] is enabled.

members.name: SetBusLayout
members.summary: Overwrites the currently used [AudioBusLayout].

members.name: SetBusMute
members.signature: public void SetBusMute(int busIdx, bool enable)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the bus at index [param bus_idx] is muted.

members.name: SetBusName
members.signature: public void SetBusName(int busIdx, String name)
members.summary: Sets the name of the bus at index [param bus_idx] to [param name].

members.name: SetBusSend
members.signature: public void SetBusSend(int busIdx, StringName send)
members.summary: Connects the output of the bus at [param bus_idx] to the bus named [param send].

members.name: SetBusSolo
members.signature: public void SetBusSolo(int busIdx, bool enable)
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the bus at index [param bus_idx] is in solo mode.

members.name: SetBusVolumeDb
members.signature: public void SetBusVolumeDb(int busIdx, float volumeDb)
members.summary: Sets the volume in decibels of the bus at index [param bus_idx] to [param volume_db].

members.name: SetBusVolumeLinear
members.signature: public void SetBusVolumeLinear(int busIdx, float volumeLinear)
members.summary: Sets the volume as a linear value of the bus at index [param bus_idx] to [param volume_linear]. [b]Note:[/b] Using this method is equivalent to calling [method set_bus_volume_db] with the result of [method @GlobalScope.linear_to_db] on a value.

members.name: SetEnableTaggingUsedAudioStreams
members.summary: If set to [code]true[/code], all instances of [AudioStreamPlayback] will call [method AudioStreamPlayback._tag_used_streams] every mix step. [b]Note:[/b] This is enabled by default in the editor, as it is used by editor plugins for the audio stream previews.

members.name: SetInputDeviceActive
members.summary: If [param active] is [code]true[/code], starts the microphone input stream specified by [member input_device] or returns an error if it failed. If [param active] is [code]false[/code], stops the input stream if it is running.

members.name: SwapBusEffects
members.signature: public void SwapBusEffects(int busIdx, int effectIdx, int byEffectIdx)
members.summary: Swaps the position of two effects in bus [param bus_idx].

members.name: Unlock
members.summary: Unlocks the audio driver's main loop. (After locking it, you should always unlock it.)

members.name: BusCount
members.summary: Number of available audio buses.

members.name: InputDevice
members.summary: Name of the current device for audio input (see [method get_input_device_list]). On systems with multiple audio inputs (such as analog, USB and HDMI audio), this can be used to select the audio input device. The value [code]"Default"[/code] will record audio on the system-wide default audio input. If an invalid device name is set, the value will be reverted back to [code]"Default"[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] [member ProjectSettings.audio/driver/enable_input] must be [code]true[/code] for audio input to work. See also that setting's description for caveats related to permissions and operating system privacy settings.

members.name: OutputDevice
members.summary: Name of the current device for audio output (see [method get_output_device_list]). On systems with multiple audio outputs (such as analog, USB and HDMI audio), this can be used to select the audio output device. The value [code]"Default"[/code] will play audio on the system-wide default audio output. If an invalid device name is set, the value will be reverted back to [code]"Default"[/code].

members.name: PlaybackSpeedScale
members.summary: Scales the rate at which audio is played (i.e. setting it to [code]0.5[/code] will make the audio be played at half its speed). See also [member Engine.time_scale] to affect the general simulation speed, which is independent from [member AudioServer.playback_speed_scale].

signals.name: BusLayoutChanged
signals.summary: Emitted when an audio bus is added, deleted, or moved.

signals.name: BusRenamed
signals.summary: Emitted when the audio bus at [param bus_index] is renamed from [param old_name] to [param new_name].

values.name: PLAYBACK_TYPE_DEFAULT
values.summary: The playback will be considered of the type declared at [member ProjectSettings.audio/general/default_playback_type].

values.name: PLAYBACK_TYPE_MAX
values.summary: Represents the size of the [enum PlaybackType] enum.

values.name: PLAYBACK_TYPE_SAMPLE
values.summary: Force the playback to be considered as a sample. This can provide lower latency and more stable playback (with less risk of audio crackling), at the cost of having less flexibility. [b]Note:[/b] Only currently supported on the web platform. [b]Note:[/b] [AudioEffect]s are not supported when playback is considered as a sample.

values.name: PLAYBACK_TYPE_STREAM
values.summary: Force the playback to be considered as a stream.

values.name: SPEAKER_MODE_STEREO
values.summary: Two or fewer speakers were detected.

values.name: SPEAKER_SURROUND_31
values.summary: A 3.1 channel surround setup was detected.

values.name: SPEAKER_SURROUND_51
values.summary: A 5.1 channel surround setup was detected.

values.name: SPEAKER_SURROUND_71
values.summary: A 7.1 channel surround setup was detected.

constants.name: SPEAKER_MODE_STEREO
constants.summary: Two or fewer speakers were detected.

constants.name: SPEAKER_SURROUND_31
constants.summary: A 3.1 channel surround setup was detected.

constants.name: SPEAKER_SURROUND_51
constants.summary: A 5.1 channel surround setup was detected.

constants.name: SPEAKER_SURROUND_71
constants.summary: A 7.1 channel surround setup was detected.

constants.name: PLAYBACK_TYPE_DEFAULT
constants.summary: The playback will be considered of the type declared at [member ProjectSettings.audio/general/default_playback_type].

constants.name: PLAYBACK_TYPE_STREAM
constants.summary: Force the playback to be considered as a stream.

constants.name: PLAYBACK_TYPE_SAMPLE
constants.summary: Force the playback to be considered as a sample. This can provide lower latency and more stable playback (with less risk of audio crackling), at the cost of having less flexibility. [b]Note:[/b] Only currently supported on the web platform. [b]Note:[/b] [AudioEffect]s are not supported when playback is considered as a sample.

constants.name: PLAYBACK_TYPE_MAX
constants.summary: Represents the size of the [enum PlaybackType] enum.
